neighborhoods.name: Rehavia
neighborhoods.vibe: Historic, upscale neighborhood with strong intellectual and Anglo communities. Known for grand stone villas and mature residential character.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 10-15 minutes via light rail or car
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Light rail connects to central Jerusalem business district
neighborhoods.new_construction: Limited; mostly renovations of existing period properties

neighborhoods.name: Talbiya
neighborhoods.vibe: Upscale, tree-filled neighborhood with Mediterranean charm. Popular with wealthy Anglos seeking traditional Jerusalem character and proximity to cultural institutions.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 15 minutes by car or light rail
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Good access to business district via light rail and main roads

neighborhoods.name: German Colony (Katamon)
neighborhoods.vibe: Charming historic neighborhood with Templar-style architecture, vibrant cafe culture, and strong Anglo community. The heart of Jerusalem's expat scene.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 15 minutes by light rail or car
neighborhoods.new_construction: Boutique mixed-use developments along Emek Refaim

neighborhoods.name: Baka
neighborhoods.vibe: Bohemian, artistic neighborhood adjacent to German Colony with similar cultural appeal but slightly more residential character. Popular with creative professionals and families.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Walking distance to German Colony employment and services
neighborhoods.new_construction: Boutique residential projects with underground parking

neighborhoods.name: Katamon (Old Katamon)
neighborhoods.vibe: Established Modern Orthodox community with architectural charm. Family-oriented neighborhood with strong educational institutions and Anglo presence.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 15 minutes by car or light rail

neighborhoods.name: Arnona
neighborhoods.vibe: Suburban-style neighborhood south of the city center with good schools and family amenities. More spacious than central neighborhoods with growing Anglo presence.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Bus and car commute to central Jerusalem
neighborhoods.new_construction: Active development with new apartment complexes

neighborhoods.name: Mekor Haim
neighborhoods.vibe: Modern Orthodox neighborhood with established religious institutions and strong Anglo immigrant community. Family-oriented and community-focused.

neighborhoods.name: Talpiot
neighborhoods.vibe: Suburban neighborhood south of city center with value pricing and good infrastructure. Mix of secular and religious residents.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Bus commute to central business district
neighborhoods.new_construction: Urban renewal projects near light rail expansion

neighborhoods.name: Har Homa
neighborhoods.vibe: New development with modern elevator buildings offering good value. Mix of families and young professionals appreciating newer construction and protected rooms.
neighborhoods.new_construction: Active new residential developments with modern standards

neighborhoods.name: City Center
neighborhoods.vibe: Urban neighborhood combining modern commerce with historic character. Central location appeals to professionals seeking walkable downtown living.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: Walking distance or 5 minutes by light rail

neighborhoods.name: Neve Tzedek
neighborhoods.vibe: Polished, upscale historic neighborhood with European charm, restored buildings, and cultural attractions. Premium location combining beach access with urban sophistication.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Short commute to central business district
neighborhoods.new_construction: Limited; preservation of historic character with selective renovations

neighborhoods.name: Old North
neighborhoods.vibe: Tree-filled residential village with tree-lined streets near Hilton Beach. Popular with expats for its quiet charm, good schools, and beach proximity.

neighborhoods.name: Florentin
neighborhoods.vibe: Gritty, artistic neighborhood with raw street art, young demographics, and vibrant nightlife. Hip and affordable compared to nearby Neve Tzedek.

neighborhoods.name: Lev HaIr (City Center)
neighborhoods.vibe: Modern downtown with Rothschild Boulevard corridor featuring luxury towers and high-end amenities. Commercial and residential hub with strong international presence.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Central location, walking distance to employment

neighborhoods.name: Ramat Aviv
neighborhoods.vibe: Upscale residential neighborhood in northwest Tel Aviv with shopping mall, excellent schools, and family-oriented atmosphere. Popular with expats seeking suburban comfort.
neighborhoods.new_construction: Ongoing residential development and mall expansion

neighborhoods.name: King George Street
neighborhoods.vibe: Iconic bohemian street with international vibe, eclectic dining, and shopping. Central location combines peaceful tree-lined charm with vibrant street culture.

neighborhoods.name: Bavli
neighborhoods.vibe: Family-oriented residential neighborhood with green spaces and good schools. Quieter alternative to central Tel Aviv.

neighborhoods.name: Shapira
neighborhoods.vibe: More affordable neighborhood in southern Tel Aviv. Up-and-coming area with lower prices and developing infrastructure.

neighborhoods.name: Kiryat Shalom
neighborhoods.vibe: Working-class neighborhood with affordable pricing. Developing area with improved transportation connectivity.

neighborhoods.name: Neve Sha'anan
neighborhoods.vibe: Affordable neighborhood in south Tel Aviv with value pricing. Improving infrastructure and connectivity.

neighborhoods.name: Kfar Shalem
neighborhoods.vibe: Most affordable neighborhood in Tel Aviv with lowest per-square-meter pricing. Industrial and residential mix with improving development.

neighborhoods.name: Neve Sha'anan
neighborhoods.vibe: Young immigrant neighborhood with vibrant Modern Orthodox and Anglo community. Popular with Technion students and observant families seeking neighborhood stability.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Technion is primary employer in area

neighborhoods.name: Ahuzza
neighborhoods.vibe: Affluent Carmel neighborhood with tree-filled streets and mix of religious and secular residents. Popular with expats and international students.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Short commute to downtown and university

neighborhoods.name: Denia (Merkaz HaCarmel)
neighborhoods.vibe: Most affluent Haifa neighborhood on Mount Carmel with stunning city views and low density. Attracts affluent expats and families.

neighborhoods.name: Upper Hadar
neighborhoods.vibe: Accessible neighborhood with lower prices. Good transit access despite hilly terrain.

neighborhoods.name: Carmeliya
neighborhoods.vibe: Mixed religious-secular neighborhood on Carmel with community focus and family amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Western Carmel
neighborhoods.vibe: Residential Carmel area with nature access and family-oriented character.

neighborhoods.name: City Center
neighborhoods.vibe: Modern town center with planned development, strong Anglo community, and comprehensive amenities. Popular with young professional families.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Train station connects to Tel Aviv employment hubs

neighborhoods.name: North Neighborhoods
neighborhoods.vibe: Planned suburban neighborhoods with family focus and strong Anglo presence, particularly British immigrants.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 40 minutes to Tel Aviv by train

neighborhoods.name: South Neighborhoods
neighborhoods.vibe: Residential areas with family amenities and Anglo community presence.

neighborhoods.name: Modi'in Center
neighborhoods.vibe: Planned modern city with strong Anglo community between Jerusalem and Tel Aviv. Family-oriented with excellent schools and suburban infrastructure.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 30 minutes to Tel Aviv, 30 minutes to Jerusalem by train
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Train connects to both Tel Aviv and Jerusalem employment centers

neighborhoods.name: Western Modi'in
neighborhoods.vibe: Residential neighborhoods with family focus and improved connectivity.

neighborhoods.name: Eastern Modi'in
neighborhoods.vibe: Newer development areas with more affordable pricing.

neighborhoods.name: Ramat Beit Shemesh Aleph
neighborhoods.vibe: Vibrant Modern Orthodox Anglo hub with mature infrastructure and excellent schools. Strong community integration and religious institutions.
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Car or bus commute to Jerusalem

neighborhoods.name: Ramat Beit Shemesh Bet
neighborhoods.vibe: Newer Modern Orthodox neighborhood with family focus and developing community institutions.

neighborhoods.name: Old City (Beit Shemesh)
neighborhoods.vibe: Working-class Haredi neighborhood with lower prices. Less oriented to Anglo immigrants.

neighborhoods.name: Herzliya Pituach
neighborhoods.vibe: Ultra-luxury coastal villa belt attracting wealthy international buyers. Premium address with sea views and trophy properties.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 30 minutes to Tel Aviv by car

neighborhoods.name: Herzliya City Center
neighborhoods.vibe: Developed town center with tourism and commercial focus. More accessible than Pituach with beach and entertainment amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Ir Yamim
neighborhoods.vibe: Crown jewel of Netanya with planned community design, wide streets, and parks. Popular with upscale buyer demographic including international investors.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 35 minutes to Tel Aviv by train
neighborhoods.commute_to_employment_hubs: Train or car commute to Tel Aviv employment

neighborhoods.name: Agamim
neighborhoods.vibe: Established neighborhood with mature community and good schools. Family-oriented with mid-market pricing.

neighborhoods.name: Shirat HaYam
neighborhoods.vibe: Newest luxury neighborhood under development, positioned as ultimate destination connecting to waterfront.

neighborhoods.name: City Center
neighborhoods.vibe: Developed port city center with commercial focus. Less Anglo presence than northern cities.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 50 minutes to Tel Aviv by train

neighborhoods.name: Residential Neighborhoods
neighborhoods.vibe: Working-class neighborhoods with affordable housing and family amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Beach Neighborhoods
neighborhoods.vibe: Coastal neighborhoods with beach appeal and tourism focus. Mediterranean character with lower prices than northern beaches.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 60 minutes to Tel Aviv by bus

neighborhoods.name: City Center
neighborhoods.vibe: Developed city center with commercial activity and affordable pricing.

neighborhoods.name: Yud Alef Neighborhood
neighborhoods.vibe: Most affordable major city in Israel with value pricing and improving infrastructure. Student housing due to Ben-Gurion University.

neighborhoods.name: Park HaNahal
neighborhoods.vibe: More affluent neighborhood with luxury villas and riverside amenities. Highest-priced area in Beer Sheva.

neighborhoods.name: Kalaniyot Neighborhood
neighborhoods.vibe: Upscale neighborhood with villa-style homes and amenities.

neighborhoods.name: City Center
neighborhoods.vibe: Developed city center with commercial and residential mix. Industrial city undergoing modernization with improved infrastructure.
neighborhoods.commute_to_city_center: 20 minutes to Tel Aviv by train or car
